results.check: Manifest schema 2.1 separates package identity from three exact runtime identities
Every runtime manifest entry matches its CLI SKILL_VERSION, CLI BUILD_ID, and protocol asset
Member 1.8.1 task validation accepts ai-sop-member-skill-v2.1.0 as its package_path without changing runtime identity
Embedded Member 1.8.0 runtime validates exact legacy assignments without referencing an older root package
Existing Member 1.8.1 assignment acceptance and predevelopment behavior remain preserved
Existing Member 2.0.0 development acceptance, confirmation, scope, stale detection, and submission behavior remain preserved
Version-preflight documentation selects exactly one runtime and never substitutes the package version for a task runtime
V2.1.0 independent regression suite: 20 tests, zero failures and zero errors

known_boundaries: Package version 2.1.0 is a distribution identity and never satisfies a task that requires runtime 1.8.0, 1.8.1, or 2.0.0 by itself.

A-C tasks bind exact runtime and package provenance in the assignment; the current D-E 2.0 task contract binds runtime name/version/build while repository runtime lock and manifest protect package provenance.
The legacy_predevelopment runtime is compatibility-only and must not be selected for new 1.8.1 tasks.
Version preflight only reminds and blocks; it never silently installs, upgrades, downgrades, or globally overwrites the local Skill.
Member submission confirmation has gate_effect none and never authorizes Gate approval, merge, release, or production action.
Windows validation of UTF-8 Chinese SKILL.md requires Python UTF-8 mode, for example PYTHONUTF8=1.
